title: Gently Spicy Indian Mincemeat Palak Paneer
description: A spicy and tender Indian dish featuring mincemeat in a palak paneer form, cooked to perfection in a bamboo steamer. This ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly recipe is perfect for a romantic dinner.
instructions: Rinse the spinach and blanch it in boiling water for 30 seconds to set the color.
Shock the spinach in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Squeeze out as much water as possible from the spinach and puree it in a blender or food processor.
Cut the paneer into small cubes and set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon of ghee or oil in a pan over medium heat and saute the cumin seeds until fragrant.
Add the mincemeat to the pan and cook until it is browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the garam masala, coriander powder, cayenne pepper, and salt to the pan and stir to combine.
Stir in the spinach puree and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the mixture is heated through.
Add the paneer cubes to the pan and stir gently to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a bamboo steamer lined with parchment paper or a clean cotton cloth.
Brush the top of the mixture with the remaining 1 tablespoon of ghee or oil.
Steam the mixture over boiling water for 10-12 minutes, until the paneer is tender and the mixture is heated through.
